# UC-22.50.13 · SWIFT CSCF 6.1 — malware protection across the SWIFT secure zone

## Description

Confirms every SWIFT-secure-zone host is running malware protection with current signatures, evidencing CSCF 6.1.

## Value

CSCF 6.1 is a mandatory control; continuous coverage checks produce annual KYC-SA attestation evidence on demand.

## Implementation

(1) Identify the SWIFT secure zone with a 'zone=swift' asset field; (2) route EDR logs through a dedicated source; (3) schedule daily. Indexes required: `index=swift_zone`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=av`, `sourcetype=edr`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Endpoint detection and response (EDR), antivirus logs from the SWIFT secure zone, CSP attestation evidence..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Identify the SWIFT secure zone with a 'zone=swift' asset field; (2) route EDR logs through a dedicated source; (3) schedule daily.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=swift_zone earliest=-24h
| search (sourcetype="av" OR sourcetype="edr")
| stats count AS events count(eval(signature_coverage_hours <= 24)) AS current_coverage count(eval(action="block")) AS blocks BY host, edr_vendor
| eval up_to_date=if(current_coverage=events, "yes", "no")
| where up_to_date="no" OR blocks > 0
```

Understanding this SPL

**SWIFT CSCF 6.1 — malware protection across the SWIFT secure zone** — CSCF 6.1 is a mandatory control; continuous coverage checks produce annual KYC-SA attestation evidence on demand.

Documented **Data sources**: Endpoint detection and response (EDR), antivirus logs from the SWIFT secure zone, CSP attestation evidence. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: swift_zone.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=swift_zone, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, edr_vendor** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **up_to_date** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where up_to_date="no" OR blocks > 0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.

## SPL

```spl
index=swift_zone earliest=-24h
| search (sourcetype="av" OR sourcetype="edr")
| stats count AS events count(eval(signature_coverage_hours <= 24)) AS current_coverage count(eval(action="block")) AS blocks BY host, edr_vendor
| eval up_to_date=if(current_coverage=events, "yes", "no")
| where up_to_date="no" OR blocks > 0
```

## Visualization

Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `blocks`, `current_coverage`, `edr_vendor`, `events`, `host`, `up_to_date`.
